answer:
The harp is typically diatonic, with pedals allowing for accidentals but not all chromatic notes easily.  
The C diatonic scale (①) and Ab lydian mode (②) are playable as glissandos, while the DVII7 chord in a minor harmonic (③) and D7 in G major (④) require notes outside the standard tuning.  
The chromatic scale (⑤) is not playable as a glissando on a standard harp.  

\boxed{A}
